preamble: The shift from the neutral schwa [ə] to the near-open front vowel [æ] is a common form of re-stressing. In stress-timed languages like English, many words alternate between a reduced 'weak' form (used in fluent speech) and a full 'strong' form (used for emphasis or in isolation).

examples.note: Restoration of the full vowel when the word is stressed for emphasis.
Dialectal tendency to shift stress forward and restore full vowel qualities in prefixes.
